lede: A look at February 22 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Harry Brook and Lee Na-young, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Twosday
events.desc: Twosday, the name given to Tuesday, February 22, 2022, at 2:22:22, occurs.

events.title: 2012 Buenos Aires rail disaster
events.desc: A train crash in Buenos Aires, Argentina, kills 51 people and injures 700 others.

events.title: 2011 Christchurch earthquake
events.desc: New Zealand's second deadliest earthquake, the 2011 Christchurch earthquake, kills 185 people.

events.title: 2011 Bahraini uprising
events.desc: Bahraini uprising: Tens of thousands of people march in protest against the deaths of seven victims killed by police and army forces during previous protests.

events.title: Securitas depot robbery
events.desc: The Securitas depot robbery was the UK's largest heist. Almost £53m (about $92.5 million or €78 million) was stolen from a Securitas depot in Tonbridge, Kent.

events.title: Samuel Byck
events.desc: Samuel Byck attempts to hijack an aircraft at Baltimore/Washington International Airport with the intention of crashing it into the White House to assassinate Richard Nixon, but commits suicide after being wounded by police.

events.title: Lee Petty
events.desc: Lee Petty wins the first Daytona 500.

events.title: Yankee Clipper (flying boat)
events.desc: Yankee Clipper crashes while landing on the Tagus in Lisbon, killing 24.

events.title: War of the Austrian Succession
events.desc: War of the Austrian Succession: The Battle of Toulon causes several Royal Navy captains to be court-martialed, and the Articles of War to be amended.

events.title: Robert II of Scotland
events.desc: Robert II becomes King of Scotland, beginning the Stuart dynasty.

births.name: Hipólito Mejía
births.line: Dominican politician, 52nd President of the Dominican Republic

deaths.text: George Jellicoe, 2nd Earl Jellicoe, English politician, Leader of the House of Lords
John Donnelly (rugby league), Australian rugby league player
Charles Blondin, French tightrope walker and acrobat

observances.text: Birthday of Scouting and Guiding founder Robert Baden-Powell and Olave Baden-Powell, and its related observance: Founder's Day or "B.-P. day" (World Organization of the Scout Movement)
Birthday of Scouting and Guiding founder Robert Baden-Powell and Olave Baden-Powell, and its related observance: World Thinking Day (World Association of Girl Guides and Girl Scouts)
Christian feast day: Feast of the Chair of Saint Peter (Roman Catholic Church)
